title: impatiens aliciae var. pandavaramalayensis
intro: Impatiens aliciae var. pandavaramalayensis, also known as Pandava's Impatiens, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the Balsam family (Balsamaceae). It is native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been reported to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Its leaves are used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, and are also believed to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, offering potential health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health and immune system function.
